title: RuledSurfaceFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing Ruled Surface features in a component and supports the ability to create new Ruled Surface features.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a RuledSurfaceFeatureInput object that defines the input needed to create a ruled surface feature. Use the input object to define the input to create the desired feature and then use the Add method, passing in the RuledSurfaceFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified ruled surface feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified RuledSurface feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of RuledSurface features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Ruled Surface Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new ruled surface feature.

add.title: RuledSurfaceFeatures.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the newly created RuledSurfaceFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: An RuledSurfaceFeatureInput object that defines the desired RuledSurface feature. Use the createInput method to create a new RuledSurfaceFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the RuledSurfaceFeatureInput object) to define the desired options for the ruled surface feature.

classType.title: RuledSurfaceFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: RuledSurfaceFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a RuledSurfaceFeatureInput object that defines the input needed to create a ruled surface feature. Use the input object to define the input to create the desired feature and then use the Add method, passing in the RuledSurfaceFeatureInput object.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = ruledSurfaceFeatures_var.createInput(profile, distance, angle, ruledSurfaceType)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = ruledSurfaceFeatures_var.createInput(profile, distance, angle, ruledSurfaceType, direction)

createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created RuledSurfaceFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: A Profile object that defines the sketch geometry or edges that define the shape of the ruled surface. The Component.createBRepEdgeProfile method is useful to create a profile defined from edges.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: ValueInput object that defines the extension distance of the Ruled Surface..

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: ValueInput object that defines angle to use when creating the Ruled Surface. When the input is a real value, the units are radians.

parameters.name: ruledSurfaceType
parameters.description: The Ruled Surface type (TangentRuledSurfaceType, NormalRuledSurfaceType, or DirectionRuledSurfaceType).

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: If the ruled surface type is DirectionRuledSurfaceType, you must specify the direction. The direction is specified by providing a linear or planar entity. For example, a linear edge, construction axis, planar face, or construction plane can be used as input.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

item.title: RuledSurfaceFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified ruled surface feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: RuledSurfaceFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified RuledSurface feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: RuledSurfaceFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of RuledSurface features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = ruledSurfaceFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: RuledSurfaceFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = ruledSurfaceFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: RuledSurfaceFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = ruledSurfaceFeatures_var.objectType
